title: About - Liz Kendall MP - Liz Kendall
headings: h2: I'm Liz Kendall, the MP for Leicester West.
text_excerpt: About - Liz Kendall MP - Liz Kendall Skip to main content Liz Kendall Working hard for Leicester West Join Get Involved Donate About My Role Local News National News Parliamentary Business Contact Other help and advice Archive More Menu About I'm Liz Kendall, the MP for Leicester West. I have been the Labour MP for Leicester West since 2010. Since 2025, I have been Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology. Prior to this, I was Secretary of State for Work and Pensions. Before Labour came into government in 2024, I had been the Shadow Secretary of State for Work and Pensions. Prior to becoming an MP, I held a number positions in the think-tank and charities sector. I was Director of The Maternity Alliance (now Maternity Action), a national charity that promotes the health and well-being of pregnant women, their partners and young children. In this role, I campaigned for improved maternity and paternity rights, and more rights to flexible working. I have also been Director of the Ambulance Service Network, for whom I worked alongside patients’ groups and NHS staff to ensure full access to good emergency care services. I also held the position of Associate Director of the Institute for Public Policy Research, where I led on health, social care and children’s early years, and I was also a researcher at the King’s Fund where I focused on measures aimed at tackling local health inequalities.
